trends.name: Oil Supply Uncertainty & Iran Conflict
trends.summary: Oil prices volatile around $96-100/barrel as Iran geopolitical tension persists. Trump's vague timeline ('when I feel it in my bones') combined with Iran approving Indian LPG tankers creates conflicting signals. U.S. temporary 30-day Russian crude license through April 2026 adds complexity to supply dynamics. Market seeking clarity on duration and severity of supply disruption.

trends.name: GDP Growth Collapse & Stagflation Risk
trends.summary: Q4 GDP revised down to +0.7%, with Goldman Sachs lowering Q1 tracking to 3.2% (down 0.1%). Core PCE inflation remains sticky at +0.36% monthly, while core durable goods orders miss expectations. Recession odds surge to 36% on Kalshi, up 15 points in weeks. Stagflation concerns now dominate macro outlook.

trends.name: Recession Probability Spike
trends.summary: Kalshi prediction market now shows 36% probability of U.S. recession in 2026, up nearly 15 percentage points in recent weeks. This marks a significant repricing of economic tail risk and reflects trader concerns about weakening growth and persistent inflation pressures.

trends.name: Negative Market Feedback Loop with Oil
trends.summary: Goldman Sachs hedge fund strategists warning that stock market remains stuck in negative feedback loop with oil prices. As oil spikes on geopolitical concerns, it reignites inflation fears and growth slowdown concerns, pressuring equities. Suggests investors should seek portfolio protection. Creates systemic volatility trap.

trends.name: AI Agent Disruption & Labor Market Risk
trends.summary: ServiceNow CEO warns that AI agents could push college graduate unemployment above 30%, signaling accelerating technology-driven job displacement. This reflects broader market concern about AI adoption timelines and labor market hollowing in white-collar sectors. Adds social/political risk dimension to AI investment thesis.

trends.name: Defensive Stocks Losing Hedge Status
trends.summary: Healthcare and consumer staples—traditionally defensive sectors—have failed to provide downside protection in current market environment. This signals either market distress spreading across all sectors or defensive thesis breakdown. Investors losing traditional portfolio hedging mechanisms amid macro uncertainty.

trends.name: Fed Policy & Powell Criminal Probe
trends.summary: DOJ appeals judge's decision to block Federal Reserve subpoenas in Powell criminal probe. Ongoing legal battle over Fed independence and Powell's personal conduct adds institutional uncertainty. Could impact Fed credibility and monetary policy decisions if escalates.

trends.name: Mortgage Rates Surge, Housing Market Stress
trends.summary: Mortgage rates hit highest level since September, pressuring spring housing market at critical seasonal moment. Combined with GDP weakness and rising recession odds, housing demand likely to deteriorate. Impacts consumer balance sheets and discretionary spending heading into Q2.

trends.name: Elon Musk Leadership Crisis & xAI Exodus
trends.summary: Elon Musk orders second round of job cuts at xAI as co-founder exodus continues amid company 'rebuild' demands. Signals instability in his AI venture at critical moment for tech sector competition. SpaceX IPO timing also questioned. Adds uncertainty to Musk's tech empire trajectory.

trends.name: Trump Tariffs & Corporate Bonus Implementation
trends.summary: Policy debate over Trump tariff refunds: should companies return savings to workers as bonuses/raises or retain as margin expansion? Reflects tension between administration wage-push rhetoric and corporate profit incentives. Has implications for inflation trajectory and consumer spending.

trends.name: Nasdaq Rule Change Increases Stock Market Volatility Risk
trends.summary: Little-noticed Nasdaq proposed rule could increase risks in market for world's hottest stocks (likely high-growth/AI names). Suggests potential liquidity or circuit-breaker changes affecting the mega-cap tech names that have driven market leadership. Could amplify selloff severity if risk-off triggers.

trends.name: IRS & Tax Refund Processing Delays
trends.summary: Average IRS tax refund up 10.6% but paper check policy changes are triggering delays for 830,000+ filers. Impacts consumer cash flow timing and near-term spending. Combined with mortgage rate surge and recession fears, reduces consumer financial flexibility entering Q2.
